---
title: "AI Readiness Scorecard"
subtitle: "Operationalising the proposal's core claim — the CRM as the foundation for AI readiness"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Dashboards <span class="sep">›</span> AI Readiness Scorecard
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**Supports** the thesis **H-CRM-AI** — is the CRM the foundation for AI readiness? Context in
[Working Paper › AI Readiness](../working-paper/06-ai-readiness.qmd).
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-grade-d">Confidence D → C</span>
<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">Synthetic data</span>
<span class="pc-next">Related: [Framework](../frameworks/index.qmd) · [Evidence Register](../evidence/evidence-register.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

> **Illustrative, not validated.** Scores use *synthetic* customer data and the
> [AI Readiness Index](../frameworks/index.qmd), where **data quality** carries the heaviest weight.
> The underlying claim — that CRM/data maturity predicts AI value — is **Hypothesis H-CRM-AI** and is
> not yet validated. This scorecard exists to make that thesis *measurable*, so it can later be tested
> against real outcomes.

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Imports and project path bootstrap"
import sys, pathlib
_root = pathlib.Path.cwd()
while _root != _root.parent and not (_root / "src").is_dir():
    _root = _root.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from IPython.display import HTML

from src.utils import load_sample
from src.scoring import calculate_ai_readiness_score, calculate_customer_fit_score

PLOTLY_TEMPLATE = "plotly_white"
```

```{python}
#| label: score
#| code-summary: "Load customers and compute readiness + fit scores"
customers = load_sample("customer_profiles.csv").copy()
customers["ai_readiness_score"] = customers.apply(calculate_ai_readiness_score, axis=1)
customers["customer_fit_score"] = customers.apply(calculate_customer_fit_score, axis=1)

def band(score):
    if score >= 66:
        return "High"
    if score >= 40:
        return "Medium"
    return "Low"

customers["readiness_band"] = customers["ai_readiness_score"].map(band)
ranked = customers.sort_values("ai_readiness_score", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: metrics
#| code-summary: "Build summary metrics (computed, not hardcoded)"
n = len(customers)
mean_readiness = customers["ai_readiness_score"].mean()
high_n = int((customers["readiness_band"] == "High").sum())
high_pct = 100 * high_n / n
top = ranked.iloc[0]

def metric(label, value, sub=""):
    sub_html = f'<div class="metric-sub">{sub}</div>' if sub else ""
    return (f'<div class="metric"><div class="metric-label">{label}</div>'
            f'<div class="metric-value">{value}</div>{sub_html}</div>')

HTML(
    '<div class="metric-row">'
    + metric("Customers scored", n, "synthetic profiles")
    + metric("Mean AI readiness", f"{mean_readiness:.1f}", "out of 100 (illustrative)")
    + metric("High-readiness share", f"{high_pct:.0f}%", f"{high_n} of {n} customers")
    + metric("Most ready", top["company_name"], f'score {top["ai_readiness_score"]:.1f}')
    + "</div>"
)
```

## Customer readiness table

```{python}
#| label: table
cols = [
    "customer_id", "company_name", "industry", "employee_band",
    "ai_readiness_score", "readiness_band", "customer_fit_score",
    "data_quality", "analytics_maturity", "workflow_maturity", "ai_readiness",
]
ranked[cols]
```

## Readiness distribution

```{python}
#| label: distribution
fig = px.histogram(
    customers,
    x="ai_readiness_score",
    nbins=8,
    labels={"ai_readiness_score": "AI readiness score (0–100)"},
    template=PLOTLY_TEMPLATE,
    color_discrete_sequence=["#2f6bb0"],
)
fig.update_layout(height=340, bargap=0.08, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=20, b=10),
                  font=dict(family="Inter, system-ui, sans-serif", size=13))
fig.show()
```

## Readiness vs fit

Do the customers most *ready* for AI also *fit* the CRM best? This scatter frames that question; the
answer is an empirical matter, not something to assert.

```{python}
#| label: scatter
fig = px.scatter(
    customers,
    x="customer_fit_score",
    y="ai_readiness_score",
    color="readiness_band",
    hover_name="company_name",
    size="data_quality",
    labels={
        "customer_fit_score": "Customer fit score (0–100)",
        "ai_readiness_score": "AI readiness score (0–100)",
        "readiness_band": "Readiness",
    },
    category_orders={"readiness_band": ["High", "Medium", "Low"]},
    color_discrete_map={"High": "#2f8158", "Medium": "#b07a1e", "Low": "#6b7684"},
    template=PLOTLY_TEMPLATE,
)
fig.update_layout(height=440, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=20, b=10),
                  font=dict(family="Inter, system-ui, sans-serif", size=13))
fig.show()
```

## Maturity profile of the customer base

```{python}
#| label: maturity
maturity_cols = ["current_crm_maturity", "workflow_maturity", "data_quality",
                 "analytics_maturity", "ai_readiness"]
profile = customers[maturity_cols].mean().reset_index()
profile.columns = ["dimension", "mean_score_0_10"]
fig = px.bar(
    profile,
    x="mean_score_0_10",
    y="dimension",
    orientation="h",
    range_x=[0, 10],
    labels={"mean_score_0_10": "Mean (0–10)", "dimension": ""},
    template=PLOTLY_TEMPLATE,
    color_discrete_sequence=["#2f6bb0"],
)
fig.update_layout(height=320, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=20, b=10),
                  font=dict(family="Inter, system-ui, sans-serif", size=13))
fig.show()
```

## Interpretation

- `data_quality` is the load-bearing input of this index because the proposal asserts AI value flows
  from the data/CRM foundation.
- A wide readiness distribution is *useful*: it gives the eventual pilot enough spread to detect
  whether readiness actually predicts AI value.
- Readiness and fit are scored independently — treat any apparent relationship in the scatter as a
  question to test, not a finding.

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
## The claim under test
The heavy `data_quality` weight makes this index a restatement of **Hypothesis H-CRM-AI (grade
D → C)**. Validating it requires linking readiness scores to *actual* AI outcomes in a pilot — see
[Working Paper Ch. 6](../working-paper/06-ai-readiness.qmd).
:::

::: {.callout-warning appearance="simple"}
## Limitations
- Synthetic customers only; scores exercise the machinery, they do not describe a real book of
  business.
- The index *encodes* the thesis rather than testing it — avoid circular reasoning when validating.
:::
